inputs.fcf_source: FY2025 common distributions $285.654M / 211.667M weighted avg units (10-K cash flow stmt)
inputs.book_source: FY2025 total assets $1,335.554M / 212.334M units (GAAP; minerals at depletion cost, not fair-value floor)
inputs.normalization_note: Lawrence uses cash distributions to common unitholders, not GAAP net income ($1.28/unit FY2025).

bear.notes: Oil/gas price trough; distributions fall; working-interest capex drains cash

base.notes: Flat distributions near FY2025 run-rate; lease bonus variable

bull.notes: Gas-heavy revenue mix holds; lease bonus and working-interest participation add growth

nav_overlay.note: 17.7M net undeveloped mineral/royalty acres not separately fair-valued on GAAP balance sheet; overlay context only in base Lawrence path
nav_overlay.base_terminal_rationale: Undeveloped acreage conversion uncertain; Lawrence base uses distributions only

optionality_gate.secondary_label: Distribution yield vs undeveloped acreage option

paths.label: Bear scenario (Lawrence)
paths.weight_why: Downside stress; low weight because bear is a sensitivity, not a competing explanation of base mechanics.
weight_falsifiers: Bear assumptions become base case in filings (e.g. sustained volume collapse)
paths.hard_to_vary: yes: asymmetric low weight is structural

paths.label: Bull scenario (Lawrence)
paths.weight_why: Upside stress probe; higher than bear because optionality archetype has skew, but still below primary paths.
weight_falsifiers: Bull growth sustained in filings for 4+ quarters without falsifier trigger

deutsch_checks.reach: Weights encode evidence tier (A primary → D external), not fitted to hit a target IRR

synthesis.notes: Capstone IRR combining filings, segments, overlays, third-party, qualitative
